Despicable Me 3 (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $264,624,300Details
International Box Office $768,185,357
Worldwide Box Office $1,032,809,657
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $22,248,066
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $27,191,826
Total $49,439,892
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Balthazar Bratt, a former child star who’s grown up to become obsessed with the character he played in the ‘80s, proves to be Gru’s most formidable nemesis to date.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$72,434,025 (27.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.65 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:25.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$75,000,000 (worldwide box office is 13.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,529 opening theaters/4,535 max. theaters, 7.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $352,832,403

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 30th, 2017 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: June 14th, 2017 (Wide) (Philippines)
June 15th, 2017 (Wide) (Thailand)
June 15th, 2017 (Wide) (Singapore)
June 15th, 2017 (Wide) (Malaysia)
June 16th, 2017 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: November 21st, 2017 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for action and rude humor.
(Rating bulletin 2472 (Cert #51018), 4/19/2017)
Running Time: 90 minutes
Franchise: Despicable Me
Cast, crew, or production detail: Child Actor, Directing Yourself, Intertitle, Prologue, Same Actor, Multiple Roles
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Hollywood
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, Fired, Heist, Revenge, Robot, Secret Agent, Separated at Birth, Sibling Rivalry, Step-Family, Twins
Social setting: Adopted Family
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Time period setting: 1980s
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Illumination Entertainment, Universal Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Balthazar Bratt, a former child star who’s grown up to become obsessed with the character he played in the ‘80s, proves to be Gru’s most formidable nemesis to date.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$72,434,025 (27.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.65 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:25.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$75,000,000 (worldwide box office is 13.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,529 opening theaters/4,535 max. theaters, 7.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $352,832,403

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 30th, 2017 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: June 14th, 2017 (Wide) (Philippines)
June 15th, 2017 (Wide) (Thailand)
June 15th, 2017 (Wide) (Singapore)
June 15th, 2017 (Wide) (Malaysia)
June 16th, 2017 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: November 21st, 2017 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for action and rude humor.
(Rating bulletin 2472 (Cert #51018), 4/19/2017)
Running Time: 90 minutes
Franchise: Despicable Me
Cast, crew, or production detail: Child Actor, Directing Yourself, Intertitle, Prologue, Same Actor, Multiple Roles
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR

Source:Original Screenplay

Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
